title: orchis morio var. picta
intro: Orchis morio var. picta, also known as the painted lady's slipper or painted bee orchid, is a variety of the lady's slipper orchid (Orchis morio) and is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in European folk herbalism, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Orchis morio var. picta are attributed to its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a diuretic and to treat skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, with the dried roots and tubers being used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
